description: Concrete specs trigger and workflow eval definitions through Milestone 4, including the must-run validation boundary pack and one pinned cryptoli real-repo workflow case.

trigger_evals.goal: Exercise specs' trigger boundary on missing or stale repo-truth situations.

cases.prompt: This repo has code and tests but no AGENTS.md or specs/. Bootstrap the missing repo-truth layer so future planning and execution are safe.
cases.why: Repo truth is missing, so specs owns the bootstrap.

cases.prompt: The parser behavior changed and its unit tests are stale. Update the coverage and run the relevant suite.
cases.why: Executable truth drift belongs to tests.

cases.prompt: Add one missing field to evals/runtime.json and stop.
cases.why: A bounded implementation task belongs to execute.

trigger_evals.goal: Protect specs' ownership of repo truth without absorbing planning or review work.

cases.prompt: The repo added evals/runtime.json and .tmp/evals/, but AGENTS.md and README.md still describe the old layout. Sync the docs with code reality.
cases.why: Repo-wide operational truth is stale, so specs owns the sync.

cases.prompt: Update plans/2026-04-14-phase-05-evaluation-harness.md with the latest milestone status and blockers.
cases.why: Task-local plan maintenance belongs to plan.

cases.prompt: Review this AGENTS.md diff and tell me whether it introduced stale claims.

workflow_evals.goal: Judge whether specs can bootstrap a minimal but usable repo-truth layer from actual repo topology.

optional.why: Use this only when skill-vs-no-skill output clarifies whether the specs skill materially improves repo-truth bootstrap quality over an unguided answer.

scenario.user_request: Bootstrap AGENTS.md and specs/ for a repo that currently has neither.
repo_state: The repo has apps/api, packages/shared, tests/, dist/, and node_modules/.
There is no durable operational guidance yet.
The bootstrap should stay minimal and avoid a speculative doc forest.

assertions: Inspects the actual repo topology before writing guidance.
Creates the minimum AGENTS.md and specs/ truth needed for safe future work.
Keeps generated or vendor paths out of the owning guidance.
Names the owning paths and boundaries a fresh agent will need.
review_questions: Does the result improve agent navigability without turning into generic filler?
Does it preserve the distinction between durable repo truth and task-local plans?

workflow_evals.goal: Judge whether specs can sync stale repo truth after a structural or workflow change without broadening scope.

optional.why: Use this only when skill-vs-no-skill output clarifies whether the specs skill limits the sync to stale owning docs instead of broadening scope.

scenario.user_request: Sync AGENTS.md and README.md after the eval harness layout changed.
repo_state: The repo now tracks evals/runtime.json and skill-local eval definitions.
AGENTS.md and README.md still describe the old layout.

assertions: Reads the existing truth layer before editing.
Updates only the owning docs that are actually stale.
Keeps AGENTS.md concise and repo-operational rather than task-specific.
Reports any remaining ambiguity or structural risk honestly.
review_questions: Does the sync stay proportional instead of broadening into unrelated documentation work?

workflow_evals.goal: Judge whether specs can sync repo truth on the pinned cryptoli monorepo without mistaking generated or operational paths for owning source.

optional.why: Use this only when skill-vs-no-skill output clarifies whether specs improves multi-app repo-truth sync quality on a real monorepo fixture.

scenario.user_request: Sync cryptoli's repo-truth docs so a fresh agent can navigate the three-app pnpm workspace, the root specs, and the ops surface without treating generated output as owning truth.
repo_state: The pinned fixture includes apps/backend, apps/frontend, apps/admin, root AGENTS.md and README.md, specs/, ops/README.md, and local .agents/skills/ docs.
The evaluation branch intentionally leaves repo-truth docs stale after the admin and ops surfaces grew.
Generated and vendor paths still exist and should be excluded from owning guidance unless explicitly relevant.

assertions: Reads the actual repo topology before editing the truth layer.
Updates only the owning docs that need sync instead of editing product code or task plans.
Captures the backend, frontend, and admin app boundaries plus the root specs and ops surfaces.
Keeps generated, vendor, and copied-artifact noise out of the owning guidance.
review_questions: Does the sync improve agent navigability on the real repo without broadening into generic filler?
